mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal robust economic activity and corporate hiring, leading to higher aggregate earnings expectations and positively driving equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: A positive 6-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the headline inflation index (CPIAUCSL) is at an elevated level, it signals persistent pricing pressure that will force the central bank to maintain or initiate restrictive monetary policy, increasing the discount rate for equities and causing negative returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 230, the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly lower than when CPIAUCSL is below 230.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent downturn in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, signaling an imminent recession and causing a risk-off sentiment that drives equity prices lower.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: In periods where both proxies show a 3-month decline, the S&P 500 exhibits positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) growth serves as a leading indicator of a cyclical peak in economic activity, negatively impacting investor sentiment and future earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient remains below 0.03 during periods identified as late-cycle by NBER recession indicators.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate provides an accommodative liquidity environment, reducing corporate borrowing costs and lowering the risk-free rate used to discount future cash flows, thereby boosting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% is not statistically different from the average return when it is above 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a short period is a strong real-time indicator of a cyclical downturn, triggering immediate investor flight to safety and causing sharp, short-term declines in the S&P 500.
failure_condition: Instances where the UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp in 3 months are not followed by a negative average 5-day S&P 500 return.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms a robust economic foundation, which translates into higher realized corporate earnings and supports a positive trend in equity markets over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly real GDP growth and the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 forward return is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment environment, rising inflation creates a stagflationary scenario. This combination severely constrains monetary policy options, increases economic uncertainty, and negatively impacts corporate margins, leading to poor equity returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500's average 60-day forward return during periods meeting the trigger condition is not statistically significantly lower than its long-term average.
